ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-23-2021 Continued To Dec-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
John Boessenecker, Esq. for Daniel Reichling;
No other appearances on this matter.

Mr. Boessenecker provided a brief update stating that he has part of the petition prepared, that there is a waiver as to St. Cecilia's Church in SF, that there are $30,000.00 in unlcaimed funds with the state controller, and that on the 10th of this month that he sent in a supplemental inventory and appraisal to the court for filing.

At the conclusion of Mr. Boessenecker's comments, the Court contined the status on close of the estate to December 28, 2021, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Chan. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Jun-22-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jun-22-2020 Continued To Aug-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Randall Boessenecker appeared by telephone for Michael Reichling.

The Court informed Counsel that the declaration that was filed does not comply with Probate Code 12201 and questions what he knows about the $193,000.00 annuity. Mr. Boessenecker stated that they have been dealing with the annuity company and trying to locate various assets that the Executor wrongfully took, and that the $18,000.00 and the $48,000.00 annuities had already been paid out and taken. Mr. Boessenecker also stated that it took up and until late last year to figure out what the Executor had stolen and to communicate with the annuity company, that there was a delay due to the pandemic, that they got the claim forms and they are with the Executor, that he expects to submit the claim forms to the annuity company within the next week or two, and requested that the matter be put over for 60 to 90-days. Status on close of estate is continued to August 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has to filed by then and that he needs to get this in order. The Court also expressed concern about the health of the Executor. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

The status hearing is also continued to that date.

The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration (subject to review of the order). The Petition for Surcharge and for Recovery of Estate Property, etc. is continued to August 14, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Michael Reichling (Successor Executor).

The Court asks Counsel if she has any issue with providing representation to the current Administrator given the issues that have occurred with her former client/former administrator, stated that the current client will need to sue the former client and questions how she can represent them, questions whether probate was opened for Donald Niemer, stated that it has no problem with the Beneficiaries receiving distribution and expressed concern about approving statutory fees at this point. Ms. Harrington stated that they would not be suing as an individual, but in the roll of Administrator, that they could hire independent litigation Counsel, that she does not have a family tree for Donald Niemer, that Philip provided bank statements that show no assets and that there is no probate open for Donald. Ms. Harrington also stated that it is very complicated, that Donald died in Marin County, that the original thought was to do an investigation as to whether there were any assets, that she thinks it was several hundreds of thousands of dollars, and made mention of some annuities having been inventoried. In addition, Ms. Harrington stated that she has worked on this case for many years and hasn't been paid, that she opened two probates, that she did extensive work on genealogy, that she still needs to obtain a number of documents to complete the accounting and that she thinks she can have it resolved within the next couple of weeks. The Court stated that it will see what it can do with regard to Ms. Harrington's fees, but it is not inclined to approve any Administrator's statutory fees. The Petition for Second Preliminary Distribution, etc. is under submission for the Court to consider the Attorney's statutory fees. The Petition to Approve Status of Administration is also under submission. Status on close of estate is set for April 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Michael Reichling (Successor Executor).

Ms. Harrington stated that the prior Executor passed away, that upon the Successor Executor getting the financial records it appears the deceased original Executor did take several hundreds of thousands of dollars from the Estate, that they are in the process of getting all of the records to figure it out and that the person handling the deceased Executor's estate indicated that $5,000.00 was the only asset on hand at the time of death. Ms. Harrington also stated that there is no bond in this Estate, that there is $1,000,000.00 on hand, that the only beneficiary is Saint Cecilia's, that her thought is to file a second petition for preliminary distribution to give $750,000.00 to Saint Cecilia's, that the petition is drafted and signed, and that she is prepared to file it today. The Court reminded Ms. Harrington that at the last hearing she was told that if the petition for final distribution was not ready to be filed, she needed to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration. Ms. Harrington stated that the petition she is filing includes a report and a request for continued administration, and that they only received information on the annuity this week. Status on close of estate is continued to September 12, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 by which time said petition or another petition is to be filed.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Continued To Oct-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington, for petitioner Donald Nemir.
Ms. Harrington advised the Court that the petitioner has now located and hired a professional geneaologist to locate additional heirs. She stated that this would be a priority project, and should be completed within two months. She stated that both the decedent and her only sibling died childless, but that their father had nine brothers and sisters last known to be living in Italy, making the research project to locate additional legal heirs more difficult. The hearing on the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to October 5, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated its concern regarding the length of time the matter has been pending, and stated that no further continuances would be granted.
